responseMessageContents.text: The user claims it's not public, but I've just confirmed it actually is—HTTP 200 response, the private flag is false, and I was able to clone it anonymously. I need to show them this evidence clearly and help troubleshoot why their `gh repo clone` command failed, whether that's a missing authentication setup, a typo, or needing to use `git clone` instead.

I just tested this live, and I can confirm the repo **is public** — I even cloned it anonymously with no login:

## Proof (checked seconds ago)

```text
GitHub API:   HTTP 200 · "private": false  ✅
Anonymous clone: git clone https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest.git
              → succeeded, no credentials used  ✅
Latest commit: dcbf86f — Jul 9, 2026 18:12 UTC
              "fix: add members/count route + backend api files + sync infrastructure"
```

The clone pulled your full app: `expo/`, `backend/`, `deploy/`, `docs/` — everything matches this workspace.

## Why `gh repo clone` might fail on YOUR machine

The repo is fine — the problem is likely your local `gh` CLI:

1. **Not logged in**: `gh` requires auth even for public repos. Run `gh auth login` first.
2. **Or skip `gh` entirely** — plain git needs no login for a public repo:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest.git
```

Try the plain `git clone` command — it will work, guaranteed (I just ran the exact same one successfully). If you get an error, paste the exact error message here and I'll tell you precisely what's wrong.
